options.name: --config-file
options.description: Specify the configuration file to use, overrides the normal configuration file resolution

options.name: --config
options.description: Override specific configuration values

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

name: -V
--version
options.description: Print version information

options.name: -n
options.description: Skip loading wezterm.lua

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start the GUI, optionally running an alternative program

options.name: --cwd
options.description: Specify the current working directory for the initially spawned program

options.name: --class
options.description: Override the default windowing system class. The default is "org.wezfurlong.wezterm". Under X11 and Windows this changes the window class. Under Wayland this changes the app_id. This changes the class for all windows spawned by this instance of wezterm, including error, update and ssh authentication dialogs

options.name: --workspace
options.description: Override the default workspace with the provided name. The default is "default"

options.name: --position
options.description: Override the position for the initial window launched by this process

options.name: --no-auto-connect
options.description: If true, do not connect to domains marked as connect_automatically in your wezterm configuration file

options.name: --always-new-process
options.description: If enabled, don't try to ask an existing wezterm GUI instance to start the command.  Instead, always start the GUI in this invocation of wezterm so that you can wait for the command to complete by waiting for this wezterm process to finish

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

name: -o
--ssh-option
options.description: Override specific SSH configuration options. `wezterm ssh` is able to parse some (but not all!) options from your `~/.ssh/config` and `/etc/ssh/ssh_config` files. This command line switch allows you to override or otherwise specify ssh_config style options

options.name: --class
options.description: Override the default windowing system class. The default is "org.wezfurlong.wezterm". Under X11 and Windows this changes the window class. Under Wayland this changes the app_id. This changes the class for all windows spawned by this instance of wezterm, including error, update and ssh authentication dialogs

options.name: --position
options.description: Override the position for the initial window launched by this process

options.name: -v
options.description: Enable verbose ssh protocol tracing. The trace information is printed to the stderr stream of the process

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

options.name: --baud
options.description: Set the baud rate.  The default is 9600 baud

options.name: --class
options.description: Override the default windowing system class. The default is "org.wezfurlong.wezterm". Under X11 and Windows this changes the window class. Under Wayland this changes the app_id. This changes the class for all windows spawned by this instance of wezterm, including error, update and ssh authentication dialogs

options.name: --position
options.description: Override the position for the initial window launched by this process

--help

options.description: Override the default windowing system class. The default is "org.wezfurlong.wezterm". Under X11 and Windows this changes the window class. Under Wayland this changes the app_id. This changes the class for all windows spawned by this instance of wezterm, including error, update and ssh authentication dialogs

options.description: Override the default workspace with the provided name. The default is "default"

--help

options.name: --text
options.description: Explain which fonts are used to render the supplied text string

options.name: --list-system
options.description: Whether to list all fonts available to the system

options.name: --rasterize-ascii
options.description: Show rasterized glyphs for the text in --text using ascii blocks

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

options.name: --key-table
options.description: In lua mode, show only the named key table

options.name: --lua
options.description: Show the keys as lua config statements

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

options.name: --class
options.description: When connecting to a gui instance, if you started the gui with `--class SOMETHING`, you should also pass that same value here in order for the client to find the correct gui instance

options.name: --no-auto-start
options.description: Don't automatically start the server

options.name: --prefer-mux
options.description: Prefer connecting to a background mux server. The default is to prefer connecting to a running wezterm gui instance

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

options.name: --format
options.description: Controls the output format. "table" and "json" are possible formats

--help

--help

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: move-pane-to-new-tab
subcommands.description: Move a pane into a new tab

options.name: --pane-id
options.description: Specify the pane that should be moved. The default is to use the current pane based on the environment variable WEZTERM_PANE

options.name: --window-id
options.description: Specify the window into which the new tab will be created. If omitted, the window associated with the current pane is used

options.name: --workspace
options.description: If creating a new window, override the default workspace name with the provided name.  The default name is "default"

options.name: --new-window
options.description: Create tab in a new window, rather than the window currently containing the pane

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: split-pane
subcommands.description: Split the current pane. Outputs the pane-id for the newly created pane on success

options.name: --pane-id
options.description: Specify the pane that should be split. The default is to use the current pane based on the environment variable WEZTERM_PANE

options.name: --cells
options.description: The number of cells that the new split should have. If omitted, 50% of the available space is used

options.name: --percent
options.description: Specify the number of cells that the new split should have, expressed as a percentage of the available space

options.name: --cwd
options.description: Specify the current working directory for the initially spawned program

options.name: --move-pane-id
options.description: Instead of spawning a new command, move the specified pane into the newly created split

options.name: --horizontal
options.description: Equivalent to `--right`. If neither this nor any other direction is specified, the default is equivalent to `--bottom`

options.name: --left
options.description: Split horizontally, with the new pane on the left

options.name: --right
options.description: Split horizontally, with the new pane on the right

options.name: --top
options.description: Split vertically, with the new pane on the top

options.name: --bottom
options.description: Split vertically, with the new pane on the bottom

options.name: --top-level
options.description: Rather than splitting the active pane, split the entire window

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: spawn
subcommands.description: Spawn a command into a new window or tab. Outputs the pane-id for the newly created pane on success

options.name: --pane-id
options.description: Specify the current pane. The default is to use the current pane based on the environment variable WEZTERM_PANE. The pane is used to determine the current domain and window

options.name: --window-id
options.description: Specify the window into which to spawn a tab. If omitted, the window associated with the current pane is used. Cannot be used with `--workspace` or `--new-window`

options.name: --cwd
options.description: Specify the current working directory for the initially spawned program

options.name: --workspace
options.description: When creating a new window, override the default workspace name with the provided name.  The default name is "default"

options.name: --new-window
options.description: Spawn into a new window, rather than a new tab

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: send-text
subcommands.description: Send text to a pane as though it were pasted. If bracketed paste mode is enabled in the pane, then the text will be sent as a bracketed paste

options.name: --pane-id
options.description: Specify the target pane. The default is to use the current pane based on the environment variable WEZTERM_PANE

options.name: --no-paste
options.description: Send the text directly, rather than as a bracketed paste

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

subcommands.name: imgcat
subcommands.description: Output an image to the terminal

options.name: --width
options.description: Specify the display width; defaults to "auto" which automatically selects an appropriate size.  You may also use an integer value `N` to specify the number of cells, or `Npx` to specify the number of pixels, or `N%` to size relative to the terminal width

options.name: --height
options.description: Specify the display height; defaults to "auto" which automatically selects an appropriate size.  You may also use an integer value `N` to specify the number of cells, or `Npx` to specify the number of pixels, or `N%` to size relative to the terminal height

options.name: --no-preserve-aspect-ratio
options.description: Do not respect the aspect ratio.  The default is to respect the aspect ratio

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: set-working-directory
subcommands.description: Advise the terminal of the current working directory by emitting an OSC 7 escape sequence

name: -h
--help
options.description: Print help information

subcommands.name: record
subcommands.description: Record a terminal session as an asciicast

--help

options.description: Explain what is being sent/received

--help

options.name: --shell
options.description: Which shell to generate for

--help
